message.content:
```python
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import csv

url = 'https://www.amazon.com/s?k=laptop'
headers = {
    'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36'
}

def scrape_amazon():
    response = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
    soup = BeautifulSoup(response.content, 'html.parser')
    products = soup.find_all('div', {'data-component-type': 's-search-result'})
    
    data = []
    for item in products:
        name = item.find('h2', {'class': 's-product-image-container'}).find('span', {'class': 'a-text-normal'}).text.strip()
        price = item.find('span', {'class': 'a-offscreen'}).text.strip()
        rating = item.find('span', {'class': 'a-icon-alt'}).text.strip() if item.find('span', {'class': 'a-icon-alt'}) else 'N/A'
        data.append([name, price, rating])
    
    return data

def save_to_csv(data):
    with open('products.csv', 'w', newline='', encoding='utf-8') as file:
        writer = csv.writer(file)
        writer.writerow(['Name', 'Price', 'Rating'])
        writer.writerows(data)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    data = scrape_amazon()
    save_to_csv(data)
```

Note: Ensure you have the necessary libraries installed (`requests` and `beautifulsoup4`). You can install them using `pip install requests beautifulsoup4`.
